Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-16 11:07 a.m.
I have a remote repeater (several hops away) that I can't get the Home Assistant integration to recognize/talk to/query stats for. What are my best opitons for getting it working? (edited)
awol 2025-04-16 11:52 a.m.
I have been running into this quite a bit in general and in my experience its not related to the HA or meshcore pythong implementation itself. If I disconnect the node from HA and use the CLI to try to manually log in I find that I can't
11:52 a.m.
I sometimes lose all ability to log into a remote repeater until that repeater is rebooted
11:53 a.m.
I think it has something to do with the clock management between the client and repeater
11:53 a.m.
which kind of seems like the achilles heel of the network atm
11:54 a.m.
BUT I would check your clock, and try to use the cli to directly login to the repeater to see if that works
Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-16 12:44 p.m.
great background and suggestions, thank you
12:47 p.m.
this is a remote node that takes some effort to get to, and it appears OTA rebooting isn't getting executed, maybe related?
awol 2025-04-16 12:51 p.m.
it definitely might be, in my experience I have had luck with flashing or using a fresh node that has never logged into the repeater before
12:51 p.m.
and using that to reboot
Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-16 12:52 p.m.
I just sent an OTA clock sync command and it sync'd (didn't send an error) which suggests that maybe the node clock has fallen out of sync?
12:53 p.m.
it's a RAK that's been online for only about two weeks
12:54 p.m.
running 1.4.1
awol 2025-04-16 12:54 p.m.
when this happens to a repeater I notice I also stop getting advert updates, so I suggest the clock dropped out on the repeater.
12:54 p.m.
raks are terrible at time management they have no real clock mechanism
Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-16 12:54 p.m.
physical access isn't super easy, takes some effort
12:54 p.m.
also good to know, thanks!
awol 2025-04-16 12:54 p.m.
ordered some of these to improve the situation: https://store.rakwireless.com/products/rtc-module-rak12002?srsltid=AfmBOopc_5LxM28IA_83w_hbO8LoitNpQTI1zOiHN3Ex7J_oMAjE43GR
12:55 p.m.
I actually have this situation with one of my repeaters right now, gonna flash a new node and see if that fixes it
1
Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-16 12:55 p.m.
my thinking was once the HA integration gets connected can do a daily time sync via HA automation
12:56 p.m.
push time sync to the fleet until (if?) that becomes a core MC firmware feature
12:56 p.m.

awol 2025-04-16 1:00 p.m.

1:01 p.m.

1:01 p.m.

awol

awol 2025-04-16 1:02 p.m.

CrashUK 2025-05-01 8:59 a.m.
how to add helect v3 to the wifi network?
FDLamotte 2025-05-01 10:57 a.m.
you need to bake a firmware from platformio, there is a wifi target where you can customize the ssid and password (the IP will be given by the router so you'll have to get it from there)
CrashUK
can this monitor battery staus of remote nodes
Mike / N2QDK 2025-05-01 11:14 a.m.
Yes - but I’m having issues with monitoring nodes >0 hops
awol 2025-05-01 11:49 a.m.
Ya remote node monitoring can be spotty, works best if you have a really reliable connection to it. I’ve also run into a bug where my node stops being able to login to remote repeaters both in and out of HA. Hoping to make some general reliability improvements soon with the new MeshCore py lib
1
1
FDLamotte
you need to bake a firmware from platformio, there is a wifi target where you can customize the ssid and password (the IP will be given by the router so you'll have to get it from there)
CrashUK 2025-05-01 2:09 p.m.
I dont how to do that.
FDLamotte 2025-05-02 2:51 a.m.
This room is not too crowed so I assume I can try to give you some insights here ...
2:52 a.m.
  • start by installing vscode https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/setup/setup-overview
  • then install platformio plugin
2:54 a.m.
  • then you'll have to clone meshcore repository in your computer from here : https://github.com/ripplebiz/MeshCore
  • if you don't know how to use git, you can get a zip (click on "code")
2:57 a.m.
  • you should have a dir with meschore code in it, from vscode open that dir File -> Open Folder
  • platformio will recognize it's a platformio project, scan it and download all dependencies (this might take some time)
3:00 a.m.
  • you can start opening the platformio.ini file for your target (here for heltec_v3), and edit the WIFI_SSID and WIFI_PWD lines
3:01 a.m.
you'll then want to build your target, in the status line at the bottom, click on current target to choose heltec_v3
3:02 a.m.
plug your board and click on the arrow, it should compile the firmware and upload it to the board
3:03 a.m.
you might have to choose the correct upload port (it should be set to ttyUSB0 for an heltec_v3 on linux) but leaving it to auto should be fine as long as you have only one board hooked to your computer
CrashUK
I dont how to do that.
dit dit 2025-05-02 12:08 p.m.

BenCos18 2025-06-13 2:58 p.m.

BenCos18 2025-06-15 12:10 p.m.
well I just discovered I can control home assistant stuff using it lol (edited)
12:10 p.m.
alias: Turn on WLED when MeshCore message is test triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: sender_name: sendernamehere message: triggerword trigger: event actions: - action: input_button.press target: entity_id: input_button.setup_toggle data: {} mode: single for example this automation would be able to turn on my pc setup lol
1
Mike / N2QDK 2025-06-15 12:17 p.m.
Hahah behold the power of Home Assistant
BenCos18 2025-06-15 12:48 p.m.
haha now I just need to figure out how to make it work only in direct messages lol
awol 2025-06-15 12:54 p.m.
you can filter on message_type: direct
BenCos18 2025-06-15 12:55 p.m.
yep got it I think just before you replied lol alias: Turn on WLED on MeshCore direct message mode: single triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: sender_name: BCOS message: pc message_type: direct trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: | {{ trigger.event.data.pubkey_prefix == 'c55b6b7f0ebc' }} actions: - data: name: MeshCore Trigger message: Direct message 'pc' received from BCOS action: logbook.log - target: entity_id: light.wled action: light.toggle data: {} should have probly asked before going reverse enginnering stuff edited as this works better lol (edited)
12:56 p.m.
and yes I used chatgpt to help with the states stuff
awol 2025-06-15 3:51 p.m.
Claude made a good portion of the integration so that checks out
Mike / N2QDK 2025-06-16 8:41 a.m.

Feature Request: Expose message content as an attribute for each sensor or as an event on the home assistant event bus. I'd like to respond to incoming messages sent to the host client connected to home assistant via an automation - a set of conditionals in an automation could become the underpinnings for a bot
Uncle Lit
is there a way to do some calculation between uptime and airtime to get an average utilization number over time?
awol 2025-06-26 3:50 p.m.
There should be a way to make this math work, I can take a look at a sensor for that. Seems like someone that we would want baked into the integration given how the math would have to work
awol
There should be a way to make this math work, I can take a look at a sensor for that. Seems like someone that we would want baked into the integration given how the math would have to work
Uncle Lit 2025-06-26 3:52 p.m.
That would be awesome!
Mike / N2QDK
Feature Request: Expose message content as an attribute for each sensor or as an event on the home assistant event bus. I'd like to respond to incoming messages sent to the host client connected to home assistant via an automation - a set of conditionals in an automation could become the underpinnings for a bot
awol 2025-06-26 3:52 p.m.
Not sure I follow this, maybe I mis understanding but the message content is in the event and automations can be made around the content. This is an automation trigger I have to respond to messages that contain a given value triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: direct trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: "{{ trigger.event.data.message.strip().lower() == '!meshstats' }}"
Mike / N2QDK 2025-06-26 3:55 p.m.
will give that a shot, thanks!
Mike / N2QDK 2025-06-26 4:05 p.m.
was having trouble accessing the event
awol 2025-06-27 1:45 p.m.
I have an experimental airtime utilization sensor running and it seems to work well:
1:47 p.m.
basically ((Current airtime - Previous airtime) ÷ (Current uptime - Previous uptime)) × 100 = Utilization on every update
2

mannkind 2025-08-05 11:44 a.m.
I use the MeshCore integration in HA w/a BT Proxy. This is my current patch to MeshCore. It disables the pin if it is set to the default PIN. Long ago when I was pairing my MT client and an RPI CM4, I had to pair it manually first via the CLI before I could actually use it in my scripts. (edited)

awol 2025-08-24 5:49 p.m.
Just released version 2.1.0 Telemetry System New telemetry tracking system - Complete implementation of device and client telemetry monitoring Telemetry sensors - Added comprehensive telemetry sensors for monitoring mesh network performance Self telemetry tracking - Integration can now track its own telemetry data Client telemetry tracking - Monitor individual client device telemetry Periodic session refresh - Telemetry tracking includes automatic session management to keep connections fresh Enhanced Sensor Coverage RX sensors - New receive signal sensors for better network monitoring Rate and utilization sensors for repeaters - Monitor repeater performance with detailed rate metrics Additional command mapping - Expanded SDK command support Repeater Enhancements Improved repeater backoff logic - Relaxed exponential backoff for better reliability Fixed repeater update conflicts - Resolved issues where repeater updates would interfere with each other Enhanced repeater stability - Better handling of repeater state management User Experience Improved config flow - Enhanced configuration experience with better telemetry handling Better UX for setup process - Streamlined initial setup and configuration API & Core Enhanced meshcore-py integration - Updated to use meshcore>=2.1.2 for better compatibility Improved command handling - Added bytes support for commands Better session management - Enhanced connection stability and session handling (edited)
4
1
Wouter-Jan 2025-08-25 5:52 a.m.
Another noob-way with some additional info round repeaters and companions (in principal saving the first incoming advert) One thing for sure people forget to advert their companion once in a while.
1
Wouter-Jan 2025-08-30 5:18 a.m.
This is a way to integrate private messages (PM) with public and other channels in a tabbed card. It doesn't use any additional entities, the number of messages is unlimited, and they last for the lifetime of your logbook. This is accomplished through a small automation. The how-to can be found at: https://github.com/WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions/tree/main
GitHub - WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions: A way to manage ...

Wouter-Jan 2025-09-04 3:25 a.m.
Hi I have created this graph but was wondering if something similar can be done with Last SNR in more detail in other word split by Neighbour; where is this hidden?
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-04 11:41 a.m.
@awol What is the maximum of contacts in combination of HA? I am getting a warning in the logs of HA: Event data for meshcore_raw_event exceed maximum size of 32768 bytes. This can cause database performance issues; Event data will not be stored. This is likely caused by EventType.CONTACTS. At the moment my contact/node count is 165.
Wouter-Jan
Hi I have created this graph but was wondering if something similar can be done with Last SNR in more detail in other word split by Neighbour; where is this hidden?
awol 2025-09-04 12:20 p.m.
Last SNR is always the SNR that the node received for the status request. As for the neighbors feature, it could be queried manually with some automations, but its just a text response back right now so would have to be parsed. They are planning on refactoring the neighbors feature, and I will probably add it natively then
Wouter-Jan
@awol What is the maximum of contacts in combination of HA? I am getting a warning in the logs of HA: Event data for meshcore_raw_event exceed maximum size of 32768 bytes. This can cause database performance issues; Event data will not be stored. This is likely caused by EventType.CONTACTS. At the moment my contact/node count is 165.
awol 2025-09-04 12:22 p.m.
There is no real maximum in HA. Are you using customlfs that allows for more contacts? The contacts packet is kind of problematic in general, its so huge. Even on my personal node to my phone the sync can take a bit. As for DB performance all event data is ephemeral unless you are saving it somewhere
awol
There is no real maximum in HA. Are you using customlfs that allows for more contacts? The contacts packet is kind of problematic in general, its so huge. Even on my personal node to my phone the sync can take a bit. As for DB performance all event data is ephemeral unless you are saving it somewhere
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-04 1:04 p.m.
in 1.8.1 the max for WSL is 300 hence the question (and yes know its huge. so hence I put the blame on contacts)
awol 2025-09-04 1:06 p.m.
so the contacts will still get persisted in HA, cause the actual sensors/trackers dont use the HA event system, the HA event system is side-loaded from the sdk event stream. The meshcore_raw_event may fail but the integration should still process the contacts (edited)
awol
so the contacts will still get persisted in HA, cause the actual sensors/trackers dont use the HA event system, the HA event system is side-loaded from the sdk event stream. The meshcore_raw_event may fail but the integration should still process the contacts (edited)
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-04 1:40 p.m.
That is good to hear. I noticed this because I looked at my logs (not doing this frequently, not my favorite thing to do) and noticed this. If not used at all is there a way to suppress it?
awol 2025-09-04 1:52 p.m.
not currently, I think what I will do is add is add some logic to detect an event that is too large for the HA envelope and truncate/redact it
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-04 1:53 p.m.
Ok, BTW I slimmed down my number of contact to 103 and "problem" solved.
awol 2025-09-04 1:54 p.m.
I can always add a disclaimer to the integratin to limit max contacts to 100 or something
1:55 p.m.
I don't love the contact management in general, I want to implement some sort of auto-pruning/rotation of contacts as they come in, but things need to be in your contacts to send dms, login etc, so rotating out a repeater you are tracking for example would not be great (edited)
1:56 p.m.
Haven't fully figured out the best way to solve this natively in the integration

CrashUK 2025-09-06 6:14 p.m.
How would a I connect a companion to HA hosted on hyper-v
Cisien 2025-09-06 6:17 p.m.
either flash usb companion and pass through the usb device to HA, or pass a bluetooth adapter throuhg to HA and connect to the companion with that
awol 2025-09-06 6:22 p.m.
Depending on the node, you can also build from source and flash a Wi-Fi companion (edited)
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-07 5:39 a.m.
Easiest nodes are ESP32 starting with Heltec V3 because this one is already prepared for Wifi, next Heltec WSL similar to V3 (start from ble; comment ble lines and add wifi lines from V3), Benefit WSL is the external Wifi connector for improved Wifi reception with better antenna. Benefit ESP32 over nRF more memory for contacts.

repeaters can be remotely administered

Just released 2.1.4 which adds first class device tracking for devices with GPS telemetry
10:14 p.m.
https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/releases/tag/v2.1.4
Release 2.1.4 · meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha
Adds 1st party device tracking for tracked devices that expose GPS telemetry. GPS telemetry will be auto discovered and added to the device: Location history can easily be displayed using the map ...

Cisien
mostly because it gets weird with usb passthrough
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-08 4:30 a.m.
Not sure if this is helpful in your situation (also running everything in Docker) but with multiple USB attached to your system might swap USB ports on reboot. This is the remark I made in my compose.yml how to prevent this: # Use for USB device its ID and not the assigned USB port because when there are more than 1 USB devices attached on a # system reboot assigned USB ports can (and will) change. UDEV can be used also but requires extra work outside the container. # Command to show USB devices by ID: sudo ls -la /dev/serial/by-id # for your docker-container use a logical USB name.
  • /dev/serial/by-id/usb-FTDI_FT232R_USB_UART_AK2NEAM6-if00-port0:/dev/ttyUSB_DSMR
Wouter-Jan
Not sure if this is helpful in your situation (also running everything in Docker) but with multiple USB attached to your system might swap USB ports on reboot. This is the remark I made in my compose.yml how to prevent this: # Use for USB device its ID and not the assigned USB port because when there are more than 1 USB devices attached on a # system reboot assigned USB ports can (and will) change. UDEV can be used also but requires extra work outside the container. # Command to show USB devices by ID: sudo ls -la /dev/serial/by-id # for your docker-container use a logical USB name.
  • /dev/serial/by-id/usb-FTDI_FT232R_USB_UART_AK2NEAM6-if00-port0:/dev/ttyUSB_DSMR

It's based off the examples in here: https://github.com/WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions
Originally sent: 2025-09-09 2:44 a.m.
1:02 p.m.
Forwarded
script.muesli_ha_send_ui_message sequence: - if: - condition: template value_template: >- {{ states('text.meshcore_message') is not defined or states('text.meshcore_message') == '' }} then: - stop: "" - action: meshcore.send_channel_message data: channel_idx: "{{ channel_id }}" message: "{{ states('text.meshcore_message') }}" - action: text.set_value metadata: {} data: value: "" target: entity_id: text.meshcore_message alias: Muesli HA - Send Channel Message description: ""
Originally sent: 2025-09-09 2:45 a.m.
1:02 p.m.
Forwarded
You should be able to just find each line like: entity_id: - binary_sensor.meshcore_dcd881_ch_1_messages and replace dcd881 with your ID
Originally sent: 2025-09-09 2:47 a.m.
1:03 p.m.
Forwarded
and make sure the _ch_1_ parts match the channel_id: 1

awol 2025-09-09 3:04 p.m.
Ya, I think there is something that can be done there. When we fire a command, to a companion client we usually get get an OK or an ERROR (sometimes we get things back like a contact list or what not if its something like contacts). I think with a big of additional wiriing, we can capturing the result and render it next the the card, it could display whatever the last result of the command was
3:05 p.m.

3:05 p.m.

6:15 p.m.

mueslimaker
would you be interested in PRs to add some example dashboards to the repo, or do you want to keep those separate from the integration? (they won't be LLM generated, I promise )
awol 2025-09-09 6:22 p.m.
yes definitely, but I should probably get a docs site up for consistency and so people know where to put stuff
6:22 p.m.
You shouldn't need to interact with the underlying data coordinater python code to send commands to a repeater, you can do that via the cli command service
6:23 p.m.
send_cmd Hyperion "advert"
6:24 p.m.
send_cmd Hyperion "set name Hyperion2"
6:29 p.m.
I might not sure I actually expose the entry_id anywhere, the entry_id will be unique per companion node you have hooked up to the HA instance, normally most people only have one so it's moot (edited)
6:30 p.m.
it has to be fetched or logged out from the underlying HA system
6:32 p.m.
you can do something like this: action: notify.notify data: message: "The config entry ID for my lamp is: {{ config_entry_id('sensor.meshcore_{PUBPREFIX}_node_count_{NODE_NAME}') }}"
6:32 p.m.
will send you a push notificaiton with the id

mueslimaker 2025-09-10 2:38 p.m.

awol 2025-09-10 4:45 p.m.

4:45 p.m.
I need to create a helper entity and then load the output into, I should be able to add that pretty easily and then let folks do with it what they want
mueslimaker 2025-09-10 5:15 p.m.
Awesome. The other thing is that there isn't a logbook entity for the companion node's messages, only ones for its contacts. I'm not sure if that's exactly what you're talking about
awol 2025-09-10 5:15 p.m.
outgoing sent messages?
5:16 p.m.
Public chats and dms should show up in the same logbooks as incoming messages
5:18 p.m.
mueslimaker 2025-09-10 5:52 p.m.
No, I mean the messages from the companion node itself, which in this case would be the responses from a command (such as OK, or the value of a query). For contacts such as a repeater, they have a logbook entity where those responses show up. But there isn't one for the companion node itself (the one that's paired to HA)
awol 2025-09-10 7:23 p.m.
ah ya I got ya, everything that the client node ever receives gets emitted as an event, but I don't send it to a log book cause the amount of data would be massive, but an automation could be set up to write events to a logbook. In the case of commands, what I plan on doing is only writing to a logbook or helper if the resulting event is from a manually run command (ideally ignoring all the automated syncing commands the integration is doing in the background). The fact that command outputs from repeaters gets written to logbooks is kind of a bug masquerading as a feature. Repeater command results come back as msgs that show up as DMS from the repeater in the node event system. Kinda works out in this case though
1

awol 2025-09-12 10:52 a.m.
depends on if the contact has a path on your node. You can set that via update_contact command. I could do automatic path discovery and set it on contacts but I dont think the sdk currently returns pathing info on messages
awol
depends on if the contact has a path on your node. You can set that via update_contact command. I could do automatic path discovery and set it on contacts but I dont think the sdk currently returns pathing info on messages

oh, that's a bummer.. so it's basically flood 100% of the time, right?

awol

10:58 a.m.

awol 2025-09-12 11:00 a.m.
either use the cli command ui card that is on the basic dashboard or go into actions in dev tools and run service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: "update_contact PUBKEYORNAME 02ad" if the sdk returns paths I can try to do auto discovery and setting
awol
either use the cli command ui card that is on the basic dashboard or go into actions in dev tools and run service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: "update_contact PUBKEYORNAME 02ad" if the sdk returns paths I can try to do auto discovery and setting
FDLamotte 2025-09-12 11:18 a.m.
the sdk tells you a path has changed and you can reload your contact to get path updated
11:20 a.m.
there is also mc.auto_update_contacts that lets the sdk automatically update contacts on path change
11:24 a.m.
and another interesting feature of the companion radio firmware, there is a command to discover path, it has been implemented as res = await mc.commands.send_path_discovery(contact) you'll receive a PATH_RESPONSE event
11:26 a.m.
this has been added recently

GitHub - WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions: My Solutions for...
My Solutions for the Meshcore Integration. Contribute to WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions development by creating an account on GitHub.
Wouter-Jan 2025-09-17 4:27 a.m.
BTW I do not try to combine a message with a path, also SNR is only important if you have no own repeater in this respect. But from each contact I try to save 'fastest' path, distance, last advert and last heard, this latter because companions tents to forgets to do an advert and the integration shows them quickly as stale.

mueslimaker 2025-09-17 2:47 p.m.
I’ve found that using WiFi is the reliable method for hooking to home assistant. When I tried using USB I got disconnections like you’re describing Compiling WiFi firmware for the heltec was super easy. You just enable the packet logging flag in the platformio config file for the heltec platform, and I just edited build.sh so it only built the wanted heltec repeater WiFi firmware instead of all of them

easy enough
FDLamotte 2025-09-24 6:55 a.m.
It's already in meshcore_py if there is no key given or a name starting with #, it will put the 128 first bytes of sha256 of the name as key

It's already in meshcore_py if there is no key given or a name starting with #, it will put the 128 first bytes of sha256 of the name as key

drewzh 2025-09-24 7:53 a.m.
Can anybody spot a reason why the following automation would stop triggering recently (all other meshcore integration features working fine): alias: Meshcore Forward to Push description: Forwards MeshCore direct & channel messages to a push notification triggers: - trigger: event id: contact event_type: meshcore_message event_data: event: EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV - trigger: event id: channel event_type: meshcore_message event_data: event: EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV actions: - action: notify.mobile_app_drew data: message: >- {% set d = trigger.event.data %} {% if d.event == 'EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV' and d.channel is defined %} Channel {{ d.channel }}: {{ d.sender_name }}: {{ d.message }} {% else %} {{ d.sender_name }}: {{ d.message }} {% endif %} mode: single (edited)
7:53 a.m.
(bare in mind it's the trigger not the action that's the issue)
7:54 a.m.
I assumed an API change with the events, but I didn't spot anything in the release notes
drewzh 2025-09-24 8:27 a.m.
It turns out that maybe the event type property is no longer populated, which is my automation stopped working. I've removed this and also made some further tweaks to get it working again
awol 2025-09-24 10:02 a.m.
event type should still be there. If you use dev tools to listen to the events what are you seeing?
drewzh 2025-09-24 1:29 p.m.
event_type: meshcore_message data: message: Just testing - ignore (debugging events) sender_name: M7DZH Base channel: public channel_idx: 0 entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_ccef73_ch_0_messages domain: meshcore timestamp: "2025-09-24T13:10:56.617347" outgoing: true origin: LOCAL time_fired: "2025-09-24T12:10:56.617360+00:00" context: id: 01K5XT4HQ9VXBE8WA0VX9258CM parent_id: null user_id: null
1:30 p.m.
Fixed by using the following automation: alias: Meshcore Forward to Push description: Forwards MeshCore direct & channel messages to a push notification mode: single triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_message conditions: # Only forward incoming messages (not ones you sent) - condition: template value_template: "{{ (trigger.event.data.outgoing | default(false)) == false }}" # Must have a message body - condition: template value_template: "{{ (trigger.event.data.message | default('')) | length > 0 }}" actions: - variables: d: "{{ trigger.event.data }}" - action: notify.mobile_app_drew data: title: >- {{ 'MeshCore: ' ~ (d.channel if d.channel is defined and d.channel not in [none, ''] else 'Direct') }} message: >- {% set ch = d.channel if d.channel is defined and d.channel not in [none, ''] else none -%} {% if ch -%} [#{{ d.channel_idx | default('?') }} {{ ch }}] {{ d.sender_name | default('Unknown') }}: {{ d.message }} {%- else -%} {{ d.sender_name | default('Unknown') }}: {{ d.message }} {%- endif %} (edited)
1:30 p.m.
Sorry can’t format from my phone
Cisien 2025-09-24 1:36 p.m.
if iphone, you can long-press the ' to get a `

sequence: - if: - condition: template value_template: >- {{ states('text.meshcore_message') is not defined or states('text.meshcore_message') == '' }} then: - stop: "" - action: meshcore.send_channel_message data: channel_idx: "{{ channel_id }}" message: "{{ states('text.meshcore_message') }}" - action: text.set_value metadata: {} data: value: "" target: entity_id: text.meshcore_message alias: Muesli HA - Send Channel Message description: ""
2:50 a.m.
sequence: - if: - condition: template value_template: >- {{ states('text.meshcore_message') is not defined or states('text.meshcore_message') == '' }} then: - stop: "" - if: - condition: template value_template: "{{ target_node_name is not defined or target_node_name == '' }}" then: - action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: "\"{{ states('text.meshcore_message') }}\"" else: - action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: >- send_cmd "{{ target_node_name }}" "{{ states('text.meshcore_message') }}" - delay: hours: 0 minutes: 0 seconds: 1 milliseconds: 500 - action: text.set_value metadata: {} data: value: "" target: entity_id: text.meshcore_message - delay: hours: 0 minutes: 0 seconds: 1 milliseconds: 500 alias: Muesli HA - Send CMD Message description: "" mode: queued max: 20

MrAlders0n 2025-09-25 11:18 p.m.

1
drewzh
Hey - could you possibly share this automation with me? Thank you
MrAlders0n 2025-09-25 11:26 p.m.
This automation listens for any public message on channel 0 and checks if the sender has already been seen, either by matching an advert/contact entity or by being listed in the known-senders text helper (with a 3000-character limit). If the sender is new, it adds them to the helper so they won’t trigger again, sends a short welcome message with links, and also notifies me of the new node.
drewzh
Hey - could you possibly share this automation with me? Thank you
MrAlders0n 2025-09-25 11:29 p.m.
alias: MeshCore - Messages from New (Unknown) Node description: >- Notify once when a node on ch0 speaks but no contact entity exists and sender not in known list triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: channel channel_idx: 0 trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: >- {% set sender = trigger.event.data.sender_name | trim %} {% set matches = states.binary_sensor | selectattr('entity_id','search','^binary_sensor\\.meshcore_.*_contact$') | selectattr('attributes.adv_name','defined') | selectattr('attributes.adv_name','equalto', sender) | list %} {% set seen = states('input_text.meshcore_known_senders') | from_json(default=[]) %} {{ (matches | length) == 0 and sender not in seen }} actions: - variables: _sender: "{{ trigger.event.data.sender_name | trim }}" _seen: >- {{ states('input_text.meshcore_known_senders') | from_json(default=[]) }} _new_seen_json: "{{ (_seen + [_sender]) | unique | list | tojson }}" - target: entity_id: input_text.meshcore_known_senders data: value: "{{ _new_seen_json }}" action: input_text.set_value - data: channel_idx: 0 message: >- Welcome @[{{ _sender }}] to Ottawa MeshCore! Check out https://ottawamesh.ca and join our Discord (link on the wiki). :wave: action: meshcore.send_channel_message enabled: true - delay: hours: 0 minutes: 0 seconds: 2 milliseconds: 0 - data: channel_idx: 0 message: "@[{{ _sender }}] Join #ottawa + #testing. " action: meshcore.send_channel_message enabled: true mode: queued max: 10 (edited)
2

drewzh 2025-09-26 5:43 a.m.
Just a PSA that after switching to TCP from USB, all of my drop-out issues are resolved. I actually think the issue is caused by Unraid/Docker locking up the device when a forward is defined - if I shut down the HA container, connect the node and then spin it back up again, it seems to resolve the USB issue for a while
5:44 a.m.

11:20 p.m.
Probably this one
11:20 p.m.
It was possible for you to set a direct path, and the other side would still be stuck in flood reply mode. The fix will detect if responses are coming in as flood, and send the path back (edited)

awol 2025-09-29 5:14 p.m.

MrAlders0n 2025-09-29 11:11 p.m.
At some point, I’ll post all my automations on GitHub for the public once they’re cleaned up and tested. For now—we’re in love with this project. Thanks @awol for all your work on this! We currently use it for:
  • New companion speaks in public:
Auto-replies in public, shares our Discord and pubic channels, and logs the companion’s name in a JSON list the first time they speak to ensure it never sends the welcome message again.
  • New repeater advert:
Sends our Discord channel the repeater name, public key, and lat/lon. It also checks its internal JSON database for overlapping IDs. Same logic as above—if the public key isn’t already in the known list, it triggers.
  • Bot commands:
  • dmme: Auto DMs the user 10 times (testing).
  • ping: Responds with “pong” in public.
  • weather: Gives a short forecast for today and tomorrow in public.
  • channels: Lists the public channels we use.
 (edited)
2
Skaarji 2025-09-30 1:03 a.m.
The ping/pong automation would be interesting
MrAlders0n
At some point, I’ll post all my automations on GitHub for the public once they’re cleaned up and tested. For now—we’re in love with this project. Thanks @awol for all your work on this! We currently use it for:
  • New companion speaks in public:
Auto-replies in public, shares our Discord and pubic channels, and logs the companion’s name in a JSON list the first time they speak to ensure it never sends the welcome message again.
  • New repeater advert:
Sends our Discord channel the repeater name, public key, and lat/lon. It also checks its internal JSON database for overlapping IDs. Same logic as above—if the public key isn’t already in the known list, it triggers.
  • Bot commands:
  • dmme: Auto DMs the user 10 times (testing).
  • ping: Responds with “pong” in public.
  • weather: Gives a short forecast for today and tomorrow in public.
  • channels: Lists the public channels we use.
 (edited)

MrAlders0n
@Wouter-Jan okay so, I would assume I would get hops from RX log? I cant figure out how to decode the payload
Wouter-Jan 2025-10-01 4:52 a.m.
The number of hops is available in the message received as "path_len" in both "EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV" as "EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV", you also have SNR but that is from the last repeater which is often your own thus not really important but from RAW you can extract a grah from your direct repeaters which give a good impression of conditions. (edited)
1

Hi, posted on #development and was sent here. I have a device attached to my homeassistant at home which is working well. However I want to see the traffic where I work too so used TailScale and socat to expose another device connected to a remote Raspberry Pi to the Tailnet. Home Assistant connects ok to that device via TCP (well, with some timeouts, but I'm looking into that) but then Home Assistant's Meshcore integration starts getting very confused, with entities from my home network disappearing and generally the UI acting odd until I disable the second device. I believe there is an assumption in the code that there is only going to be one meshcore radio attached to home assistant - is this the case? (edited)
Wouter-Jan
Your companion sees only 1 path? Could be more, or you just take the first because that is the fastest? Based on the timestamp? Maybe you can share your work in progress so others will be inspired by your example and we get sort of cross-pollination of solutions.
MrAlders0n 2025-10-04 10:11 a.m.
How the MeshCore Ping Works When any packet hits the node, Home Assistant fires a meshcore_raw_event with the RX LOG — that’s the raw LoRa frame before decryption. My automation decodes that log in real time:
  • Parses the header, path length, and path bytes (each hop’s repeater ID prefix).
  • Checks the channel-hash byte — if it’s 0x11, that means it’s the Public channel.
  • Stores the timestamp, hop count, and path string into HA input helpers (meshcore_last_*). I only have it respond to ping in the public channel. Later, when someone sends(only in public ):
@[Bender] !ping and that channel message arrives (EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV), Home Assistant compares its timestamp with the most recent RX LOG entry. If they match within a few seconds and the path lengths line up, Bender replies automatically:
Pong — your ping was received in 3 hops • Path: CF→92→4E
So the bot’s reply actually reflects the path in the RXLOG it’s possible it could be wrong and grab the wrong packet of course if enough come in at the same time (edited)
10:12 a.m.
I’m working on getting into GitHub but been busy

AG / Andrew 2025-10-07 3:28 p.m.
Love these - thank you. To get the path of any channel just remove the following from the code... - condition: template value_template: |- {{ (chan_hash_hex | default('') | string | regex_replace('[^0-9a-fA-F]','')) | int(base=16) == 0x11 }} The mesh is going to get big and we should keep this out of public and somewhere like #test ideally.
MrAlders0n 2025-10-07 4:25 p.m.
Yeah for our mesh, we specified public for the bot but also, I wanted to specify a channel to keep the packet path capture more specific. But yeah every mesh is different of course and if you’re using my automation there’s a disclaimer at the top lol. I hope anyone who uses it knows a bit of what their doing and knows to ask the mesh peeps where they want these responses to exist (edited)
1
4:27 p.m.
Because the ping response relies on the time since receiving the packet within 5 seconds and it confirms the hop count is the same. The more channels the more possibility of providing an incorrect path back in the response. Honestly what I would love to do is to drop the requirement to tie the two events together and rely only on the raw event but it’s difficult to decode the public channel (even though we know the key) because HA dosnt have any use friendly crypto tools installed.
Cisien 2025-10-07 4:28 p.m.
channels have nothing to do with the path
MrAlders0n 2025-10-07 4:29 p.m.
Yeah I know but If you look at how I tie the automation together it does, it limits the chance of capturing the incorrect path from a message from another channel when I respond with ha by limiting the messages the automation processes (edited)
4:33 p.m.
My automation, every time a packet comes in, HA gets an RX log event. My automation decodes the header and saves the timestamp, hop count, and path into helpers. Then, when HA later sees a channel message that contains !ping, it compares that message’s timestamp and hop count with the most recent RX log. If they line up, it assumes that channel message came from that exact packet, so Bender (HA) replies with the hop count and the path from the helpers. If my automation listened on all channels there’s more of a chance a message comes in at same time and bender replies with a path from another channel from a message it received (edited)
4:34 p.m.
It’s not perfect buts it’s the closest way I have found so far to do what I wanted to do with HA without encrypting the message part of the rx log and relying on that instead of the channel message event (edited)
awol 2025-10-07 5:05 p.m.
we can add some rx parsing functionality upstream of HA

Wouter-Jan 2025-10-09 7:33 a.m.
There are many solutions, this is yet another one. Background when WhatsApp integration failed me after returning from holiday I tried to implement a Meshcore - Telegram connection (one-way street). Objective was to publish public messages and adverts in telegram Including map, distance and path details. The first was also recently created (by a friend) in a web-version (with MQTT and node-red) and with (to be honest) a better layout. The second could be a good alternative for ping pong because no RF is used to reply and it stimulates people to do a companion Advert Flood more often (but hopefully not to often). Is it new? Not really I am reusing stuff already published in my https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1358879320248160419/1417781335787049020 How does it look like in Telegram? See: https://t.me/+ep-R777LtC9hMjU0 (changed link to invite) PS Does anyone know how to automate to copy a graph from Home Assistant and save it as image to be published in Telegram (or WhatsApp)? (edited)

@awol I think the HA addon needs to be less aggressive with retries, and never reset a path to flood. as best I can tell, just one user is responsible for a good 60% of the mesh traffic between login, request and response traffic as seen by cougar. A good chunk of the traffic is properly directed with a path, but the stuff that is flooding is causing a lot of noise which appears to be leading to issues sending messages. Note the Details column:
1
8:19 p.m.
(taking direct packets out of the filter)
awol 2025-10-20 8:20 p.m.
That traffic only seems possible if someone has a refresh interval of like a minute
Cisien 2025-10-20 8:20 p.m.
if this is the person I think it is, they have many nodes
8:21 p.m.
and they have a lot of nodes on the FC prefix
8:22 p.m.

awol 2025-10-20 8:22 p.m.
I think fundamentally the mesh needs dm push telemetry, everything else is kind of shuffling stuff around

Cisien 2025-10-20 8:23 p.m.
yeah
awol 2025-10-20 8:24 p.m.

8:24 p.m.

I'd suggest using that refresh interval as a base, then if there are a lot of nodes to poll, increase it

Liam Cottle 2025-10-22 1:27 a.m.
I originally had a fixed timeout of 30 seconds in the app for fetching all contacts, but even had issues increasing it. some devices were just really slow at pushing packets. I ended up switching to a new method, where I have an initial timeout of 10 seconds, and it's reset to 10 seconds each time a contact is received. that way fetching contacts can force timeout after 10 seconds of getting nothing. this continues to work even as contact limit is increased, because you no longer have to care how long it takes to fetch all contacts, only how long it takes to fetch 1 (edited)
2
FDLamotte 2025-10-22 2:22 a.m.
I think we should wait for nexcontact or contact events in a loop until last contact or timeout ... it would even be possible to make a simple anim (just print # at each contact for instance)

dotdavid 2025-10-22 2:22 a.m.

awol 2025-10-22 12:49 p.m.
I think I am going to implement a token bucket rate limiting algo. I think it will work well since it handles the current pattern well. Has some tolerance for small. bursts (i.e. on startup) but long-medium term should average out to a global rate limit. That way I can apply this algo globally without having to do math around node count. Will have to play with the numbers, but ideally I think we would hit a global average of allowing one request every ~5 minutes (edited)
12:52 p.m.
and I would only apply this to built-in polling and messaging, wouldn't really protect against people making their own automations
12:52 p.m.
could maybe apply it in theory as well, but there is no logic layer between HA actions and the device right now, people have direct access to the commands in the sdk
12:57 p.m.
@Jade @Cisien retries happen with an exponential back off spread out evenly through the refresh interval. The backoff delay is actually calculated backwards from the refresh interval so if you have a 12hr interval the delay for each retry (capped at 5) should be: 696 s → 11m 36s 1392 s → 23m 12s 2784 s → 46m 24s 5568 s → 1h 32m 48s 11136 s → 3h 5m 36s
12:57 p.m.
so curious how many nodes are being tracked and what kind of request rate you are actually seeing
awol
@Jade @Cisien retries happen with an exponential back off spread out evenly through the refresh interval. The backoff delay is actually calculated backwards from the refresh interval so if you have a 12hr interval the delay for each retry (capped at 5) should be: 696 s → 11m 36s 1392 s → 23m 12s 2784 s → 46m 24s 5568 s → 1h 32m 48s 11136 s → 3h 5m 36s
Jade 2025-10-22 12:58 p.m.

awol 2025-10-22 12:58 p.m.

awol 2025-10-23 2:35 p.m.
I really need to change how we handle contacts. An HA connected node should have auto-add contacts off, then I can harvest and create sensors for every new contact advert received, store those and create a UI component for manually adding them to the node if you really need them added for some reason (DMs or repeater tracking).

8:05 p.m.

I think my heltec v3 died.. it's very unreliable now. I can't be sure but I swear it excessive writes with hundreds of contacts constantly coming in. Is there a straightforward way to manage HA with only a few contacts on the device? I am only interested in monitoring a couple of my repeaters. Cisien 2025-10-25 10:25 a.m.
I think there's still something up with an excessive number of requests being sent as flood. I made a change earlier in the week that stopped Cougar from repeating flooded REQ and RESPONSE packets, as a result of that my repeater (that only sees cougar and sometimes another node) and it only receives 43% of the traffic that cougar does. that missing ~57% is the dropped REQ/RESP packets.
10:26 a.m.
The addon probably shouldn't be reverting to flood, maybe when adding a repeater ask to set/verify the path
10:30 a.m.
10:30 a.m.
https://analyzer.letsme.sh/packets?repeater_a=7E7662676F7F0850A8A355BAAFBFC1EB7B4174C340442D7D7161C9474A2C9400&region=pugetmesh&repeater_b=C51DEEC07A23CE758D065FAFB3A79014E75AE0DFD9EECAFF9A9F27E055A84136
awol 2025-10-25 10:54 a.m.
Not sure what I'm looking at there, but turning off flood reset might cause even more traffic in the case a path is broken.
10:54 a.m.
Rate limiting and/or just disabling a repeater after enough failures might be a more direct fix (edited)
Uncle Lit 2025-10-25 11:19 a.m.
Cougar is my only way out. Even though cougar is not repeating the request and respond packets, it is still taking the airtime to receive them. That is making my outgoing messages very unreliable, sometimes more than 70% of my messages are not heard by cougar due to collisions. @awol I would love if you could minimize the request and respond packets.
awol 2025-10-25 11:21 a.m.
It's just hard to me to really understand what's happening here. I added 15 repeaters to my HA with a few hour refresh rates to simulate high usage and my local repeater traffic didn't really even note it
11:24 a.m.
Only thing I can do is brute force rate limit, but it's gonna be awhile before I have time to get that in
awol 2025-10-25 11:37 a.m.
I think a deeper problem is that if any actor with basic programmatic tools can bring down a local mesh, it's a bit of a problem for the project. Relying on the best behavior of all agents in the mesh is not gonna scale (edited)
11:38 a.m.
might be somewhat inherent to the technology tho (edited)
awol 2025-10-25 11:47 a.m.
gonna be a matter of time until someone uses the SDK to try and pull near real time telemetry
1
awol 2025-10-25 12:54 p.m.
The more I think about it the more I think this might be a design issue in MC, same basic problem that MT has with telemetry just from the other angle. We have replaced passive telemetry spam with active telemetry req spam. I really think we need telemetry hooks or subscriptions (edited)
12:54 p.m.
so someone can say "send me telemetry via dm every 6 hours"
12:54 p.m.
would drastically cut down on telemetry chatter

8:48 p.m.
I've got any channel working with - send_channel_message when replying to a channel request - action: meshcore.send_channel_message data: channel_idx: "{{ p.channel_idx }}" message: |- {{ joke }} This was easy and works fine - but is based on the data coming from HA being set.

awol 2025-10-27 12:40 p.m.
Ideally that wouldn't error, but it is ultimately because the connection stopped receiveing a contacts from the node so it would have been broken either way. The second error is the root cause really. Sometimes the SDK sees the connection as open but fails to read any packets. Not sure what the reason is but recreating the connection always fixes it (edited)
12:44 p.m.
Also not sure what version you are on, but in the most recent version I added a delay between reconnect attempts because I found this can happen more often when the connection is interacted with too quickly after being first created

I updated mine yesterday as well. it required a reboot of the device for the integration to see my WiFi companion.
  • delete old meshcore integration (beta wasn't listed when I tried)
  • add integration
  • reboot (not restart HA)
  • add WiFi companion to meshcore integration
  • didn't appear to work, did not find companion
  • reboot
  • add again
  • reboot
  • companion finally showed up
Basically, reboot after you add or delete something every time

AG / Andrew 2025-10-28 3:14 p.m.
I had this. A v3 would crash on connect. I think the filesystem got corrupted. Even after reflash it would MC crash when connecting over BT, TCP or USB. Meaning you could not do anything.
  • I tried erase wipe on MC website
    • flashing MT with full erase, then back to MC
  • flashing reticulum, then back to MC Nothing worked and always had the same behaviour. Then I used the ESP web tool..
https://esptool.spacehuhn.com/ I ensured to delete all partitions on the esp, then wipe. After that I flashed MC and to my surprise it worked OK. I think the key step was deleting the partitions
ESPWebTool
Flash your ESP32 or ESP8266 through your browser. Open-Source, free, and easy to use.

awol 2025-10-28 3:42 p.m.
I would try these exact steps:
  • Unplug node
  • Restart node
  • Plugin node
  • (Check if available)
  • If not available restart HA If it's still not available we probably have to consider there might be an issue in the client-node itself in which case testing if you can connect via another PC and the webapp: https://meshcore.co.uk/apps.html is a good step. I understand the frustration, going through it methodically can help diagnose the issue. If you are feeling a bit flooded atm, a break before re-engaging can be helpful

awol 2025-10-28 3:42 p.m.
It's little consolation, but it's not the upgrade that broke it, it's likely the restart/power cycle that broke the connection which might have happened the next time the device restarted (edited)

mattzz 2025-10-28 4:38 p.m.
Flaky connectivity, devices randomly disappearing etc. points in the direction of power issues. Connecting the device without battery means that USB has only to deliver power to the heltec and not charge a battery at the same time. I suspect the usb port on the device is not capable of delivering much more than 500mA if the HA green is based on an embedded board.

Jade 2025-10-28 9:33 p.m.

Uncle Lit 2025-10-28 9:50 p.m.
@awol how does the new integration works to lower flood requests? a longer time between calls?
Uncle Lit
@awol how does the new integration works to lower flood requests? a longer time between calls?
awol 2025-10-28 9:54 p.m.
Token bucket rate limiting with max of 20 that slowly refills over an hour. So over time it only lets folks make 20 requests an hour
9:54 p.m.
Everytime a request is make it tries to take a token from the bucket and if the bucket is empty it skips
9:55 p.m.
Most people will never use 20 tokens an hour but it prevents a flood of traffic (edited)
Jade 2025-10-28 9:56 p.m.

awol
Correct, it can be disabled. I think one of the core problems is when a repeater just becomes unavailable and HA forever looks for it via flood
Uncle Lit 2025-10-28 11:51 p.m.
if a user-set path request fails, don't reset or forget the path, try flood once. if flood doesn't work, wait for its next turn, and use the original user-set path again. if flood works, note that, and next time the original user-set path fails, try the flood-found path, once. if it fails, wait for the next turn again. try not to flood at all cost, use it only once to see if another path is possible.
LAZEE486 2025-10-29 2:41 a.m.
bless you for this, as soon as i manage to get my unit on the wifi will be connecting and testing...
Brins0
Click to see attachment 🖼️
mrlm 2025-10-29 6:17 a.m.
Looking in Unifi Network the main difference I can see is that my main SSID has a split config between 2.5 and 5 to allow different settings. 2.4GHz WiFi Optimisation IoT - on Multicast to unicast - off UAPSD - off Minimum Data Rate Control - auto WPA2 PMF Disabled 5Ghz Uses the same SSID and Private Preshared keys with normal Wi-Fi settings. WiFi 7 is a separate SSID with MLO on 5Ghz and 6 GHz only as it needs WPA3, it does not touch 2.4. I'm not sure any of this is needed for the ESP devices but you could try it, I have spent a lot less time looking at disconnect issues since I did it this way. (edited)
1
mrlm 2025-10-29 6:51 a.m.

WiFi 7 is a separate SSID with MLO on 5Ghz and 6 GHz only as it needs WPA3, it does not touch 2.4. LAZEE486 2025-10-29 9:21 a.m.
and yes, shared 2.4 / 5ghz ssid can have issues with esp32 wifi. if you google cheap smartplugs (which are basicly just esp32 or esp8266 modules) you'll see its quite common.. you want a 2.4ghz 20mhz wid channel with wpa2 and no fast roaming for stable iot...

awol 2025-10-29 12:59 p.m.
I was hoping to make it even more direct but without code changes it looks like this is the best it can do: In your dashboard go to add a widget, select "Vertical-stack" then click code editor and add this. type: vertical-stack cards: - type: markdown content: >- **Update Contact Path** Set a direct routing path to a contact. Format: `update_contact <pubkey_prefix> <path>` Example: `update_contact d20c9b abc123,def456` - type: entities entities: - entity: text.meshcore_command name: Command - type: button name: Execute icon: mdi:console tap_action: action: call-service service: meshcore.execute_command_ui

awol 2025-10-29 1:55 p.m.
Give it some time to stabilize if you just turned it on a couple hours ago, when the rate limiting is hit, the backoff is triggered which is proportional to refresh time, so it can take awhile to stabilize without flooding the network
1
awol
Give it some time to stabilize if you just turned it on a couple hours ago, when the rate limiting is hit, the backoff is triggered which is proportional to refresh time, so it can take awhile to stabilize without flooding the network

1:56 p.m.

1:56 p.m.

awol 2025-10-29 1:57 p.m.
out of curiousity, how many repeaters do you have added to the integration, and how many are now disabled via config vs still being tracked?
awol
Give it some time to stabilize if you just turned it on a couple hours ago, when the rate limiting is hit, the backoff is triggered which is proportional to refresh time, so it can take awhile to stabilize without flooding the network
awol 2025-10-29 2:01 p.m.
for example if you have a 12 hour refresh configured this should be the expected retry schedule give or take a bit Retry 1: +11m36s Retry 2: +34m48s Retry 3: +1h21m12s Retry 4: +2h54m00s Retry 5: +5h59m36s
Uncle Lit 2025-10-29 2:09 p.m.
I am only monitoring one repeater, and it is 30 feet from me
awol 2025-10-29 2:10 p.m.
that tracks
1
mrlm 2025-10-29 2:13 p.m.
Interesting, so as long as you don't have a blue line down near zero all the time this is fine?
2:14 p.m.
Is there an average / peak that could go on a dashboard. It took a while to find this.
awol 2025-10-29 2:15 p.m.
I think you can get that type of info by feeding the sensor data into apex-charts cards
Uncle Lit 2025-10-29 2:16 p.m.
with 20 tokens, and if you have 20 repeaters, and if their path works, you should be able to get all the stats you need for all 20 repeaters.
awol 2025-10-29 2:16 p.m.
people should only really ever hit zero if they are tracking a lot of repeaters. I am tracking 10 and I still rarely hit zero
2:17 p.m.
unreliable paths or unreachable repeaters with a shorter refresh interval can deplete the pool quite a bit tho (edited)
Uncle Lit 2025-10-29 2:19 p.m.
yes, far away repeaters are just naturally harder to connect. that is an RF problem that the HA integration can't change. what is important is not blow up the mesh because of brute force spamming requests, and that is not the case any more with 2.2.0
awol
I expose the rate limiting bucket as a sensor so you can track your limiting, you can see when I restart and need to resync everythign it quickly depletes and slowly refills
awol 2025-10-29 2:20 p.m.
for example, the stable period in this chart is the tracking of 10 reliable repeaters, you can see it does use tokens but never really goes below 10, then the last few hours are when I turned tracking back on for an unreachable repeater with a 10 minute refresh window, you can see that the retries quickly starve out the pool (edited)
2:20 p.m.
im testing the auto disabling of an unreachable repeater
2:22 p.m.

awol 2025-10-29 2:53 p.m.
It's not meant to be helpful, it's meant to protect the mesh against someone installing the integration on HA and forgetting about it for months. The timeout is going to be 7 days

Jade 2025-10-29 2:53 p.m.
okay
awol 2025-10-29 2:54 p.m.
if we find that impacts people actively using it, we can adjust the timing, but there will definitely be a lot of people with zombie HA instances over time (edited)
Jade 2025-10-29 2:54 p.m.
of course
awol 2025-10-29 2:57 p.m.
We are working within the parameters of the protocol right now. I think PULL based telemetry is good on paper, and works in the small scale. I also get why it was done to avoid the flood telemetry spam of MT. I think there really needs to be a telemetry PUSH system where you can register a destination and a path with a device and say "DM me this telemetry every X hours". That would DRASTICALLY cut down on traffic and reduce the complexity of a system like this to have to manage and scale itself for the benfit of the mesh
1
5
2:57 p.m.
That is hard with these types of devices tho, might be too much state for them to manage
2:58 p.m.
but if we can do room servers we can probably do that
3:00 p.m.
with the the current system, tracking 22 devices is always going to be painful, it's a lot of state, retries, intermittent connections to navigate, and I think really pushes the limits and demonstrates the shortcomings of PULL based telemetry

3:34 p.m.

3:35 p.m.
ideally these telemetry push packets wouldn't even require an ack, just treat it like a UDP packet (edited)

mrlm
How does the meshcore.xxxxx.discovered_contacts file work?
awol 2025-10-29 5:06 p.m.
It's a file thats automatically generated by HA when you call store (persistance) with data. Right now the integration writes to it everytime a new contact is discovered, it gets permenantly added to that file. One file gets created per MC device added, so if you add/remove the integration they get orphaned. You could definitely write a script that merges them and puts them in your latest, but you would have to do it while the integration is disabled and then re-enable it. Otherwise it will just overwrite what you did
mrlm 2025-10-29 5:07 p.m.
are the entries unique
5:07 p.m.
or could a discovery be there more than once
5:07 p.m.
like a log
Brins0 2025-10-29 5:07 p.m.
is there a simple way of pruning old contact which haven't been seen for say, a month?
awol 2025-10-29 5:08 p.m.
with the latest update I would advocate removing as many contacts as you can and only adding them as needed from the discovery list
5:08 p.m.
you can script the removal, but otherwise you can use the new UI card/service to make it a bit easier to do manually
Brins0 2025-10-29 5:08 p.m.
I mean on the HA integration, the node only has like 5 contacts on it
awol 2025-10-29 5:09 p.m.
the unavailable contacts?
5:09 p.m.
i.e. sensors for contacts that were once made but no longer exist on the node
Brins0 2025-10-29 5:09 p.m.
well old ones, when the integration starts they're all unavailable
awol 2025-10-29 5:09 p.m.
https://meshcore-dev.github.io/meshcore-ha/docs/ha/contacts#cleanup-unavailable-contacts
Contact Management | Home Assistant
The MeshCore integration uses manual contact management mode to give you full control over which devices are added to your node's contact list.

Brins0 2025-10-29 5:10 p.m.

awol 2025-10-29 5:11 p.m.

5:12 p.m.
it forces all contact management to be manually while maintaining a list of discovered nodes now
5:12 p.m.

mrlm
or could a discovery be there more than once
awol 2025-10-29 5:13 p.m.
a discovery can only be in a file once cause its all keyed by node pubkey, so it's impossible to have dupe entries
5:14 p.m.
so if you merge files you would probably just overwrite

Jade
@awol I am convinced there is some sort of issue with how the integration logs in to repeaters. This repeater is on my roof, I can log in easily from my phone and WisMesh Tag. I can log in easily when the same Heltec V3 which is used for Home Assistant is connected to my phone or the webapp. Yet more often than not the HA integration can't login 6 tries, 4 failures, 2 successes. If logins were more successful that would solve a lot of issues: (edited)
awol 2025-10-31 10:13 p.m.
Your phone client is doing retries under the hood so the math isn’t so clear (edited)
10:14 p.m.
And the phone performs back to back retries while the integration avoids that behavior and spaces them out so it feels less reliable (edited)
10:15 p.m.
If there is something fundamentally wrong with the packets it would be something to explore in the python SDK since the integration is really just an orchestration layer for that
awol 2025-10-31 10:29 p.m.
I could potentially change things to mimic the app and do 2-3 quick retries every time we try a request but I’m wary of adding more traffic since I think telemetry scales poorly on the mesh right now (edited)
10:30 p.m.
What works for human driven interactions can be a giant foot gun when automated
awol 2025-10-31 10:42 p.m.
If you really want to go down the route of more custom automation, you can completely take over the request patterns. If you "Disable repeater" in the config this will keep all the sensors in place but stop automatic requests. Then if you can make an automation that makes requests for repeater stats using this action/service: service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: 'send_statusreq <PUBKEY_PREFIX>' Since the sensor subscribe to all status responses from the node the sensors will update even when you make the request manually via this service.
1
10:43 p.m.
The amount of customization you can do with HA is both awesome and slightly terrifying lol
10:49 p.m.
for example here is an automation that sends 5 status requests at midnight automation: - id: meshcore_statusreq_midnight_5x_spaced alias: "Meshcore: 5 status requests at midnight (spaced)" mode: single trigger: - platform: time at: "00:00:00" variables: pubkey_pre: "<ABCDEF>" gap: "00:00:10" action: - repeat: count: 5 sequence: - service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: "send_statusreq {{ pubkey_pre }}" - if: - condition: template value_template: "{{ repeat.index < 5 }}" then: - delay: "{{ gap }}" (edited)
1
awol
If you really want to go down the route of more custom automation, you can completely take over the request patterns. If you "Disable repeater" in the config this will keep all the sensors in place but stop automatic requests. Then if you can make an automation that makes requests for repeater stats using this action/service: service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: 'send_statusreq <PUBKEY_PREFIX>' Since the sensor subscribe to all status responses from the node the sensors will update even when you make the request manually via this service.
Jade 2025-10-31 11:54 p.m.
Is Disable Repeater something I can do in 2.1.8?
awol 2025-10-31 11:55 p.m.
No it was added in 2.2.0 but if you are taking over the requests, 2.1.8 and 2.2.0 are basically the same, token rate limits don't apply to manual calls to get status (edited)
11:56 p.m.
So the rate limiting won't apply to you, it only applies to things the integration tries to do automatically on your behalf
1
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-01 5:12 a.m.
May I do a suggestion for "Clearing Discovered Contacts", the steps can be reduced and simplified. For starters people tents to advice a HA-restart much to quickly. This can be replaced by reloading the specific part. The steps are:
  • Rename the storage file: .storage/meshcore.<entry_id>.discovered_contacts (better safe than sorry, delete later)
  • perform action "homeassistant.reload_config_entry" for Meshcore device
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-01 9:36 a.m.
If you have 300 contacts and more than some are obsolete you can use this automation for convenience. As you can see 'stale' is used but the period is extended with your desired number of days (based upon lastmod and not last_advert because of possible incorrect clock settings on repeaters). Hope you like it.
Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions/automations/Maintenance_Remove_In...
My Solutions for the Meshcore Integration. Contribute to WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions development by creating an account on GitHub.
1
awol 2025-11-01 2:20 p.m.
That seems great, if you want you can PR additions or external links to https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/tree/main/docs/docs
meshcore-ha/docs/docs at main · meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha
Home Assistant integration for monitoring and controlling MeshCore radio networks - meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha
^mlake 2025-11-02 5:00 p.m.
I'm seeing in places that you can connect to a meshcore node va TCP.... Naturally i think as most of my ESP32 devices have wifi, I imagine that's possible, but I'm not seeing anything clear of how to do this
dotdavid 2025-11-02 5:02 p.m.
@^mlake I do this as my HA server isn't in easy bluetooth range of my companion device that I use for HA. So I have a pi that exposes the serial port over TCP port 5000 using ser2net, and MC HA (or even the phone app) can then connect to the companion device over TCP.
5:03 p.m.
The ESP devices can do the wifi thing but I think you need to build your own firmware with the wifi settings as build flags
Cisien 2025-11-02 5:12 p.m.
you need to do a custom build to set the wifi ssid/password for your companion
^mlake 2025-11-02 5:21 p.m.
ok - thanks. Those are two ways I'll try to attack it....I've successfully flashed from source code, but PlatformIO giving my grief right now..
^mlake 2025-11-02 6:10 p.m.
Thanks again for the speedy responses ! Got it working now. I'm excited
2
^mlake
Thanks again for the speedy responses ! Got it working now. I'm excited
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-03 3:41 a.m.
For TCP/IP connection the Heltec V3 or WSL3 are best suited because their platformio.ini is already prepared for wifi (maybe there are more). WSL3 needs slightly more work to prepare but have the advantage to connect an external wifi antenna.
Wouter-Jan
For TCP/IP connection the Heltec V3 or WSL3 are best suited because their platformio.ini is already prepared for wifi (maybe there are more). WSL3 needs slightly more work to prepare but have the advantage to connect an external wifi antenna.
^mlake 2025-11-03 3:43 a.m.
all I had to do was add the following and it's been working flawlessly so far: -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"'
3:49 a.m.
using another WSL3 connected via BT to companion app, I can see the messages show up in home assistant which is a pretty awesome win. Next mission: My ultimate goal is to create a sensor that reports back to home assistant. Wouter-Jan 2025-11-03 4:05 a.m.
@^mlake as soon you see it in "view telemetry" it is likely to be pick-up automatic in the MC integration; I have a RAK repeater with RAK1906 sensor and see this sensor in HA; there are flaws; this sensor was supported in Firmware: v1.7.3 (after small tweaks) but not later ones (as far as I know) and not all sensors got the correct description (due to similarity of values)

mrlm 2025-11-03 4:27 a.m.
Heltec v4 also has the WiFi variant set up but adding it to Xiao s3 (maybe also c3 c6) probably isn't much work. Start by copying the USB section in ../variants/xiao_s3_wio/platformio.ini [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_usb] rename it to [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] add the build flags ; -D WIFI_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 -D WIFI_SSID='"yourSSID"' -D WIFI_PWD='"yourPSK"' try a build of the new [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] (edited)
1
mrlm
Heltec v4 also has the WiFi variant set up but adding it to Xiao s3 (maybe also c3 c6) probably isn't much work. Start by copying the USB section in ../variants/xiao_s3_wio/platformio.ini [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_usb] rename it to [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] add the build flags ; -D WIFI_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 -D WIFI_SSID='"yourSSID"' -D WIFI_PWD='"yourPSK"' try a build of the new [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] (edited)
^mlake 2025-11-03 5:02 a.m.

^mlake 2025-11-03 5:29 a.m.
Loaded the "Heltec_WSL3_sensor" onto my device and it appears to be discovered. Where does the data actually go ?
mrlm 2025-11-03 5:44 a.m.
It was more for other people wanting to do this. Once you start making changes you need to be able keep track of those changes every time you download the source, doing it properly by making a completely new WIFI env in that variant makes it easier. Just adding those to USB makes you loose that option as they cannot currently do more than one, so you need to keep going back and commenting them out to use USB instead of just selecting it. Once you have tested this you could do a PR and ask for it adding to the code so others can benefit. (edited)
1

yep that's all I changed ➜ MeshCore git:(b92d9bd) ✗ git diff diff --git a/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini b/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini index d31648b..c1110e2 100644 --- a/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini +++ b/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini @@ -179,8 +179,8 @@ build_flags = -D MAX_GROUP_CHANNELS=8 -D DISPLAY_CLASS=SSD1306Display -D WIFI_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 - -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' - -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"' + -D WIFI_SSID='"ssid"' + -D WIFI_PWD='"password"' ; -D MESH_PACKET_LOGGING=1 ; -D MESH_DEBUG=1 build_src_filter = ${Heltec_lora32_v3.build_src_filter} (edited)
2:31 p.m.
and then FIRMWARE_VERSION=1.8.1 sh build.sh build-firmware Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi and found the bin files under .pio/build/Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi/ (edited)
mrlm 2025-11-04 2:31 p.m.
@Honza What has changed, I just did this yesterday how I always have. (edited)
2:35 p.m.
@boychicken71 Are you using vscode on windows or something else? From https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/issues/83 Here are very brief instructions which may be enough to follow assuming you are using Windows. Install VScode, install PlatformIO inside that. (let it load completely and then restart VScode). Download and extract the code zip file from https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore Open the folder with VScode. (let it load completely). Change the environment (bottom bar) to env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi (....) (let it load completely). Open variants\heltec_v3\platformio.ini with the editor Find the section for the variant; [env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi] Find the WiFi build flags -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"' Set the details for your WiFi network. Close and save the file (with the x) Backup your Meshcore config first using the app (phone, PC or web), this process will not wipe it but sometimes a full erase helps. Connect the device to the PC via the USB-C connector. Close anything else which could interfere with the serial connection such as the Chrome browser if you have used the flasher. Press the upload (->) button on the bottom bar, let it compile and then flash the device. Providing nothing has gone wrong you have now converted from BT to WiFi firmware. Back in Meshcore-HA, you use the TCP option when setting up the device but if the IP changes it is going to fail so you need something permanent to identify it. On your router (WiFi/Firewall , ISP provided internet device), you may be able to set a DHCP reservation or hostname which solves this issue. (edited)
1

mrlm 2025-11-05 7:44 a.m.
The example is to show how to use favourites and other attributes as a filter, in this case in an auto entities card. type: custom:auto-entities card: type: entities title: Favourite contacts filter: include: - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_contact attributes: added_to_node: true flags: 1 exclude: [] Other attribute options are; #Contact or Discovered added_to_node: true added_to_node: false #favourite or not flags: 1 flags: 0 #contact types node_type_str: Client node_type_str: Repeater node_type_str: Room Server (edited)
mrlm 2025-11-05 7:54 a.m.
I figured this out using the Developer tool and finding an example entity in states. Last seen is not in here so (fresh and stale) will have to be done another way (edited)
awol
oh its just flags? you can set that via sdk commands
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-05 10:44 a.m.
I explored this a little bit further and the following command works: action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: change_contact_flags '6ec999788fb9' 1 And now comes the but:
  • the value is stored as an alphanumeric value and not as an integer/numeric (you can store anything best visible in states)
  • it is therefor not uploaded to the companion due to this incorrect field
  • it is therefor not persistent; after receiving an advert from the set companion this set value will be lost.
  • a favorite flag sets through in example the Android APP is a correct integers/numeric value and not effected
Hope this is just a small change
awol 2025-11-05 10:57 a.m.
Likely just need to change how arguments are mapped from the command service https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/blob/082608f0a6acdc7f5cf90bed0697b8d34de59e78/custom_components/meshcore/services.py#L366
meshcore-ha/custom_components/meshcore/services.py at 082608f0a6acd...

10:57 a.m.

@boychicken71 Are you using vscode on windows or something else? From https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/issues/83 Here are very brief instructions which may be enough to follow assuming you are using Windows. Install VScode, install PlatformIO inside that. (let it load completely and then restart VScode). Download and extract the code zip file from https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore Open the folder with VScode. (let it load completely). Change the environment (bottom bar) to env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi (....) (let it load completely). Open variants\heltec_v3\platformio.ini with the editor Find the section for the variant; [env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi] Find the WiFi build flags -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"' Set the details for your WiFi network. Close and save the file (with the x) Backup your Meshcore config first using the app (phone, PC or web), this process will not wipe it but sometimes a full erase helps. Connect the device to the PC via the USB-C connector. Close anything else which could interfere with the serial connection such as the Chrome browser if you have used the flasher. Press the upload (->) button on the bottom bar, let it compile and then flash the device. Providing nothing has gone wrong you have now converted from BT to WiFi firmware. Back in Meshcore-HA, you use the TCP option when setting up the device but if the IP changes it is going to fail so you need something permanent to identify it. On your router (WiFi/Firewall , ISP provided internet device), you may be able to set a DHCP reservation or hostname which solves this issue. (edited)

awol
Not sure how the app pairs these together, does it parse the raw hex and compare message + timestamp to correlate?
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-08 3:09 p.m.
Hope I give the correct answer but for pairing it looks that a hash field is available; this hash field is one of the output fields in https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-packet-capture. This will link EventType.RX_LOG_DATA records together. But is it possible to link this with a EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV record or should you decode/decrypt the EventType.RX_LOG_DATA a bit further? Likely answer is No and Yes. The decode/decrypt option seems possible with https://github.com/michaelhart/meshcore-decoder. Due to my limited programming skills I got stuck here (because I prefer either docker or node-red).
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-08 3:18 p.m.
For argument sake there is in my opinion no need to rebuild ALL the Android/IOS APP functionality inside this integration; in this scenario a second 'normal' companion will do the trick to. Otherwise you can create an overkill of information by sending this data to: https://analyzer.letsme.sh/packets

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-11 3:49 a.m.
For starters a bit overkill and with your new 2.2.1 slightly redundant. But yes everything between the brackets was advert information, the path is even stored in an extra entity. Path straight from the msg is much better. Thanks for that again. Perhaps the distance is still added value however there is a downside if "mike" is not an unique adv_name (because of multiple public-keys) it will give these multiple () records until these obsolete discovered clients are removed from HA.

mrlm 2025-11-11 9:30 a.m.
Testing now, seems to be working fine but I am seeing significant slow down in HA whenever this integration is enabled. Only have main companion, two clients and two repeaters with telemetry monitored. HAOS is running on an RPi4 8GB with SSD so its not short of resources. CPU goes from averaging less than 5% normally to 25-35% when its is running. Disabling those four things in Manage Monitored Devices seems to speed it up again. (edited)
mrlm
Testing now, seems to be working fine but I am seeing significant slow down in HA whenever this integration is enabled. Only have main companion, two clients and two repeaters with telemetry monitored. HAOS is running on an RPi4 8GB with SSD so its not short of resources. CPU goes from averaging less than 5% normally to 25-35% when its is running. Disabling those four things in Manage Monitored Devices seems to speed it up again. (edited)

Honza 2025-11-11 12:35 p.m.
I have one companion and one repeater on rpi5 8gb and SD card (running HA and zigbee2mqtt in docker) $ free total used free shared buff/cache available Mem: 8257680 810576 5372976 6000 2170160 7447104 Swap: 524272 0 524272 $ w 18:33:37 up 4 days, 3:52, 1 user, load average: 0.00, 0.01, 0.00 (edited)
mrlm 2025-11-11 1:08 p.m.
What I mean is it seems like HA OS and Python that most of the code is written in, is not optimised to make use of multiple cores. So the RPI4,8GB is only ever using a fraction (1/4 cpu, 1/8 ram) of its resources. If all that is one cpu core it will cause issues despite plenty being available. (edited)
1:12 p.m.
The other issue is that my large HA install uses a few % cpu normally. This integration alone is used many times more.

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-12 9:27 a.m.
I am missing the possibility to delete a discovered contact; overtime some of the discovered records will become obsolete but it is not possible to remove them from the meshcore.<ID>.discovered_contacts. Ok, we have the blunt axe method to delete the complete file (works great BTW) but to be honest I love the concept of discovered contacts (read: initial there is no need to add them to the node) and hope for a less radical method.

6:50 a.m.

6:53 a.m.

It was never spiking, just constantly using all of one core.
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-13 7:05 a.m.
