Wouter-Jan 2025-11-21 4:43 a.m.
@mrlm I hope you are not collecting nodes (like perhaps in the MT-days). Be clad the integration gives the opportunity to go beyond the maximum number of contacts of the node with this discovery setting. If I look at my contacts overview then I can remove 21% of the repeaters because I have not received an advert in the last 3 days (checking lastmod btw to tackle incorrect clock settings). So maintenance is always a good idea. Companions are more difficult due to the notorious lake of doing adverts timely but here you can play with add to the node and even make them favorite. My question to @awol would be is it possible that the discovery "maintenance" does not rely on the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts file but can be done completely inside HA; so manual deleting obsolete discovered contacts with also the possibility to automate this?
mrlm 2025-11-21 5:12 a.m.
In the early days I was collecting nodes to see how the mesh was spreading and had them all added as contacts, this eventually got too much for the board to deal with. I have removed them all, cleaning up HA of all traces of the integration and its entities and only having a tiny number of added contacts now. After a few days I have 500 discovered again and it starts to slow other bits of HA down, obviously half the previous 1000 were old or distant nodes, which now cleaned up wont come back. If remove the discovered contacts file occasionally it it doesn't use any resources but leaves behind the contact which I will need to clean up later. I am going to rebuild the whole of HA on Proxmox as it has been running for years and use the PI for something else. (edited)

10:08 p.m.

AG / Andrew
Still fills the database with junk
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-22 3:46 a.m.
No it does not start as junk because you received an advert and a system does not know if this is a contact you want to keep or not, so if @awol turns it off or create a toggle the consequence is that you will not receive ANY new adverts as a (discovered) contact unless other preparations are made. @mrlm yes as long as they are in the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts they swap to discovered state again, but the blunt axe method deleting that file works but this will remove ALL discovered contacts. and yes it would be nice when you change a contact from discovered to stale/fresh it is deleted from this file and if you remove a contact (from your node) it will only return in this file again (and as a discovered contact) after a new advert is received. However this solves not the problem of deleting obsolete discovered contacts (unless with the detour) or the new adverts @AG / Andrew whishes to ignore.

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-22 8:17 a.m.
It looks like that the problem might not be completely HA because it looks like that the discovered file resides in memory (roundish 3 in 1 mb); when I delete this file without doing any further action, the file is recreated in its original size. For those who needs to take frequent action and give @awol some room for his changes below an automation to make this easier: alias: Meshcore - Maintenance Delete Discovered Contacts File & Reload description: "" triggers: [] conditions: [] actions: - action: shell_command.delete_discovered_contact_file data: {} - action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry metadata: {} data: {} target: device_id: 97b090d2c09320ecc86f3f564e55345d - action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts data: {} mode: single The awful device_id you need to change in your Meshcore device with all the contacts. You also need the shell_command: shell_command: delete_discovered_contact_file: mv -f /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts_old Here you need to change the folder and name of the file into your correct values. f.y.i. I am working with a dockered HA environment so I might skip some parent folder due to the command: volumes: - "/opt/homeassistant/config:/config"` in my Docker file so in your situation addressing this file might be different. The automation was now made complete with the action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts to get really rid of them. If you want to go back to the original situation that is possible with the action of two renaming files and one action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry. (so do not use this complete automation) I tested it for my situation and works like a charm (edited)
mrlm 2025-11-22 8:18 a.m.
Surely that device id is only relevant to you? Have you added an integration like "delete files and folders" and setup that shell command in in configuration.yaml? (edited)
1

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-24 7:09 a.m.
I started fresh; so with an empty discovered file, cleanup unavailable contacts, etc. Toggled global function to be sure. When receiving a new contact this contact is added as unavailable with limited details, removing this contact works like a charm (towards the discovered contact file), adding this contact will add a line "added_to_node": true (to the discovered contact file) the node itself is updated (visible in an updated node count) , but due to the limited data available data in HA the contact will remain unavailable.

10:48 a.m.

awol

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-24 11:21 a.m.
I reverted back to my old file and I notice when an advert is received the discovered contact file is updated but not the entity in HA. Same Bug? (edited)
awol 2025-11-24 11:59 a.m.
Possibly, previously the entity would check the in memory contact list for updates on every tick, this involved a lot of look up time. I moved it so it only updates when an explicit update event is received which should result in less compute usage, but its possible there are contact update edge cases where an event is not processed correctly
12:00 p.m.
I might do something where it relies on events but does a force resync every so often to correct for any drift, tbd
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-24 1:07 p.m.
Is it possible to skip the discovered contacts file entirely and work solely from inside HA. Just thumsucking.
awol 2025-11-24 1:21 p.m.
the file is the HA default way of persisting info between reloads
mrlm 2025-11-24 4:24 p.m.
My thought was to only create an entity for added contacts and avoid using entities for discovered entirely. As they are not something that gets interacted with all the time they don't need to be loaded in memory or actively being processed. Just use the file or multiple as a simple DB, that way it can scale as needed without affecting performance. (edited)

Wouter-Jan 2025-11-26 11:51 a.m.

Is that only for nodes added to the client?
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-26 5:05 p.m.
This is what is happening with complete newly discovered items; in HA they start as unavailable until you do a reload of the device_id: 405 + 14 = 419 Because I only have one contact added to my node I only can say that this one is still awaiting to go from discovered to fresh and if I manipulate that it in HA this will not last. But good news the added contact dropdown box shows correctly the added node(s) .
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-26 5:34 p.m.
Ok, here is the situation when a contact is added to the node, the dropdown box shows this contact correctly but the advert is not yet updated in HA (btw this advert was done prior to the adding to the node). It looks like that the fields; added to node and status are never updated in HA. Advert is updated in HA after a reload. But this is not a new observation
awol 2025-11-26 5:52 p.m.
gotcha, I have a fix to push that should help this
1
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-27 4:23 a.m.
First impression looks good but I noticed that contacts which were already added to the node can have a missing attribute added_to_node: true (or perhaps an incorrect one). After a reload this attribute was removed from the newly added (I check if it is gonna return). So I added an extra contact to the node to show this attribute is available in HA (temporarily?)
4:23 a.m.
To be honest it is perhaps a bit strange to have 3 values (discovered/stale/fresh) in a binary_sensor, also the quick change from fresh to stale after 12 hour does not represent for me a good indication the contact will become obsolete; 2 or 3 days for repeaters / room servers is perhaps a better indication, for companions it will be more 7 days or so due to the lack of sending adverts. So according to me contact added to node true or false is a better binary_sensor and more in line with HA. Not a request, I understand you also want to be in line with Meshcore itself and you are juggling with data to get everything in line. (edited)
mrlm 2025-11-27 6:28 a.m.
Looking good to me as well, just had to remove and re add the contacts that were added outside HA or on the last version.
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-27 7:17 a.m.
@awol Ok, did find sometime (not new, so perhaps not yet in v2.2.2) ; A new adverts first result is still a state unavailable with limited data. Found something to correct this more specific, in short a reload of this newly create entity (instead off the complete device). Most likely you will find a better solution. actions: - action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry target: entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_zaandamz_repeater_5a1cd5669896_contact data: {}
mrlm 2025-11-27 7:53 a.m.
When I initially add the contact added_to_node: true is present. After some time it has broken again and that attribute has vanished.
awol 2025-11-27 9:49 a.m.
Hmm strange
9:50 a.m.
Unfortunately I’m going to be out on a couple trips and may not have much time to debug this until later in Dec or Jan
mrlm
When I initially add the contact added_to_node: true is present. After some time it has broken again and that attribute has vanished.
awol 2025-11-27 9:51 a.m.
I would imagine a new advert is overwriting the previous state
mrlm 2025-11-27 9:59 a.m.
I don't think it is an advert as these remain the same before and after it breaks. last_advert: 1763988931 .. last_advert_formatted: "2025-11-24T12:55:31" I think it is a periodic process running. For now I can probably just change the code to not use this attribute. (edited)
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-27 10:40 a.m.
The important part is that the status is correctly reflected in HA, perhaps sometimes with a delay but that is no problem, have a good trip and thanks for all the good work done with this integration,
mrlm 2025-11-27 11:31 a.m.
I just need to use the state instead of the attribute but this isn't very clear. https://github.com/thomasloven/lovelace-auto-entities?tab=readme-ov-file#repeating-options Figured it out, I was trying exclude in the include section which it just ignored. type: custom:auto-entities filter: include: - options: {} entity_id: binary_sensor.meshcore_*_contact exclude: - options: {} state: discovered card: type: map default_zoom: null label_mode: icon focus: false grid_options: columns: full rows: 10 (edited)
awol 2025-11-27 12:30 p.m.
looks like there was a silly mistake in the sensor sync code, fix is probably easy, might be out soon if it works

trisweb 2025-11-27 11:59 p.m.
Might that have something to do with many of my repeater/client entities (that should be working) are Unavailable?
trisweb
Might that have something to do with many of my repeater/client entities (that should be working) are Unavailable?
Wouter-Jan 2025-11-28 3:46 a.m.
Is this happens first do a reload of your node device ID (or a blunt HA restart), for those who are remaining you might have a mismatch between your node and HA, if they still exist in the discovered contact file I prefer in this situation for all those unavailable records a remove_contact over remove_discovered_contact to eliminate this mismatch. Last step is cleanup unavailable contacts. If you still have a mismatch between your node count and contacts (difference +1 is ok) count a new advert of those records might solve it, but others are perhaps already inactive. I solved this outside HA with the Android APP.. Explanation: the contacts on your node will always be in HA see meshcore-dev.github.io/meshcore-ha/docs/ha/contacts#automatic-contact-syncing). (edited)
1

mrlm 2025-12-02 3:37 p.m.

I get it, my point is 0,0 is not special any more than a single number chosen in a lottery. It is a valid coordinate and should not be used for something without a location by meshcore or assumed not to have one by maps.

it's invalid by design.. as we don't have lat=undef lng=undef

CrashUK 2025-12-28 10:09 a.m.
setting ip address in router is a pain.
CrashUK
setting ip address in router is a pain.
Wouter-Jan 2025-12-28 11:23 a.m.
You do not need to have an explicit IP-address, you only need to know it. your router should give you this information and even let you bind a specific IP-address to your device. Good routers should do this but router from your ISP might have limited skills so that is causing your pain.
Wouter-Jan
You do not need to have an explicit IP-address, you only need to know it. your router should give you this information and even let you bind a specific IP-address to your device. Good routers should do this but router from your ISP might have limited skills so that is causing your pain.
CrashUK 2025-12-28 1:29 p.m.
One more thing. Can do configure the node? He has WiFi only? I reflash with Bluetooth firmware the back to WiFi firmware but that's a pain. I need to add more channels to node.
mrlm 2025-12-28 4:53 p.m.
You can stop the integration and use the app to connect via wifi temporarily. MC-HA will keep trying to connect and kick you out depending on what you have configured so I just reboot it.
4:55 p.m.
You need to have an explicit IP or hostname, there is no way around it. Otherwise MC-HA will not remain connected long term. Preferably set a DHCP reservation on your router for the device as it is most reliable. (edited)
CrashUK
I dont know how to do that?
davidneuner 2025-12-29 4:57 a.m.
you can use this tool for patching https://www.weyhmueller.org/webtools/esp32_ssid_patcher.html
dotdavid
@^mlake I do this as my HA server isn't in easy bluetooth range of my companion device that I use for HA. So I have a pi that exposes the serial port over TCP port 5000 using ser2net, and MC HA (or even the phone app) can then connect to the companion device over TCP.
👽 wazmac 2025-12-29 6:55 p.m.
I am having issues getting this same config working with ser2net. Would you mind sharing your ser2net.yaml or example for this.
👽 wazmac
I am having issues getting this same config working with ser2net. Would you mind sharing your ser2net.yaml or example for this.
dotdavid 2025-12-29 7:01 p.m.
Sure, it took a lot of fiddling around. Here's one of my device definitions connection: &dotdavidwork accepter: tcp(nodelay),5000 enable: on timeout: 0 options: kickolduser: true connector: serialdev, /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_CP2102_USB_to_UART_Bridge_Controller_0001-if00-port0, 115200n81,local
👽 wazmac 2025-12-29 7:06 p.m.
@dotdavid I added the (nodelay) and timeout: 0 to mine and it works now. Thank you muchly
1

The basic repeater statistics section is this: - type: grid columns: 2 cards: - type: custom:flex-table-card title: Repeater Statistics entities: include: sensor.meshcore_*_repeater*_battery_percentage* columns: - name: Repeater data: friendly_name modify: x.replace('Battery Percentage', '').replace('MeshCore', '').trim() - name: Battery data: state suffix: '%' align: center - name: Success data: request_successes align: center - name: Failed data: request_failures align: center - name: SNR data: last_snr suffix: ' dB' align: center - name: Path data: path modify: x.split(',').join(' → ') sort_by: friendly_name+ css: table+: 'font-size: 12px;' th+: 'background-color: var(--primary-color); color: white;' (edited)

It does show when "Add Repeater Station" but then there's a bunch of data about repeaters and the telemetry I've added is greyed out and cannot be enabled. Sensor Nodes | meshcore-dev/MeshCore | DeepWiki
Sensor nodes are specialized firmware applications that collect environmental data from attached sensors, store time-series data, and can send alerts based on configurable triggers. They do not repeat

GitHub - rgregg/meshcore-proxy: TCP proxy for MeshCore companion ra...
TCP proxy for MeshCore companion radios connected via USB Serial or BLE - rgregg/meshcore-proxy
Clinton
Anyone know if it is possible to use a MC node connected to Home Assistant, but expose it for use through other devices? There's this nifty https://github.com/rgregg/meshcore-proxy utility that sure looks like it could do that. I would want it running on my home assistant computer, because it is on all the time, but I've got no idea how I'd run the meshcore-proxy script at bootup time.
TF-Mambo 2026-02-14 5:34 p.m.
Explain what you are trying to accomplish. The MC HA integrations allows you to send and receive messages direct or channels. Any device logged into HA would have the same functionality from a single node. Keep in mind the rate limit and that there would be no simultaneous access to the node.
TF-Mambo
Explain what you are trying to accomplish. The MC HA integrations allows you to send and receive messages direct or channels. Any device logged into HA would have the same functionality from a single node. Keep in mind the rate limit and that there would be no simultaneous access to the node.
Clinton 2026-02-14 6:00 p.m.
The whole point of the proxy is that two applications could use the same node. Suppose Meshcore for Home Assistant sends a few messages every ten minutes; the rest of the time, I could use the node with other software, such as the regular mobile app, or maybe Citadel for MC or anything else. That would be the intent. Use a device for Home Assistant, but also allow it to be used for other purposes in between messages.
mrlm 2026-02-14 6:03 p.m.
Tcp proxy makes some sense for me as sometimes I disable the MC-HA integration to manage the node with the app or manage a repeater. Wifi is a lot better than BT for this but the gap has narrowed since fast sync. There is a need to understand how it would work as there can only ever be one primary device so where do replies and notifications go?
6:05 p.m.
When you connect do you become the primary client, that breaks MC-HA as it receives data all the time in the form of messages and adverts. It would now miss everything while a second client was connected unless there is a clever around this. (edited)
mrlm
When you connect do you become the primary client, that breaks MC-HA as it receives data all the time in the form of messages and adverts. It would now miss everything while a second client was connected unless there is a clever around this. (edited)
Clinton 2026-02-14 6:25 p.m.
Hmm. I thought MeshCore-HA sent out messages to repeaters every few minutes asking for their stats and then recorded it; why would it be listening to all data all the time?
mrlm 2026-02-14 6:29 p.m.
It does or can but the integration can do lots of things only limited by meshcore.py. such as receive data like the client app. I have an automation that forwards any direct or private channels messages as an android notification. I can then vpn to home and reply all via cellular or wifi anywhere in the world I have a connection. (edited)

mrlm 2026-02-19 9:52 a.m.
Meshcore-HA working with Meshrank.net https://discord.com/channels/1465838297229168925/1465839782188810546/1474056812927127693 Go to Meshrank.net Create account, Login. Go to account settings, Observer Setup. MQTT Uplink (beta), Create MQTT credentials. Go to your Home Assistant, Meshcore-HA integration, MQTT Broker Settings. Copy the settings across as shown. Debug will show the packets and a new observer will appear in Meshrank nursery for four hours. (edited)

Highlander 2026-02-21 10:29 a.m.
Hello, I have an issue with MeshCore in HA, is not able to connect to my companion node and I think the fault is the PIN on my node, but I don't find an option to disable it. How can I do it?
mrlm 2026-02-21 10:37 a.m.
BT pins don't work properly on HA, you cannot use a proxy and must pair the device first then set up what ever integration you want to use. Disabling the pin requires a custom firmware and quite a few changes. Can you use USB or WiFi instead?
mrlm
BT pins don't work properly on HA, you cannot use a proxy and must pair the device first then set up what ever integration you want to use. Disabling the pin requires a custom firmware and quite a few changes. Can you use USB or WiFi instead?

mrlm 2026-02-25 7:22 a.m.
I don't use HA in Docker as it is too limited, when I did it was unsupported but I think that may have changed. USB is probably going to be easier than BT as that may require USB/passthrough anyway. On here you can see devices being passed through so it should be possible https://community.home-assistant.io/t/home-assistant-in-docker-pass-usb-device-from-host/184674 I use HAOS in a VM but it will depend on the hypervisor, architecture and base OS whether you can do this. As I don't have access to an Arm Mac I cannot advise on what issues you may run into. (edited)

PiecesAndPawns 2026-02-25 11:25 a.m.
I have an idea I'd like to get feedback on, before deciding if it should be raised as a "feature request" via github issues. Since a lot of people use a companion for multiple things (mqtt to letsmesh and other projects, meshcore-bot, home assistant, S.A.M.E weather alerts, and several other things.. it'd be awesome if we could have a single companion node (probably tied to HA), and let the other tools use it as a TCP connection, so that we don't need 11ty7 companions. Would it be possible AND, would it be of interest to create essentially a "tcp proxy" type of service in the HA integration, so the other tools could tie into it and use the same companion?
mrlm
I don't use HA in Docker as it is too limited, when I did it was unsupported but I think that may have changed. USB is probably going to be easier than BT as that may require USB/passthrough anyway. On here you can see devices being passed through so it should be possible https://community.home-assistant.io/t/home-assistant-in-docker-pass-usb-device-from-host/184674 I use HAOS in a VM but it will depend on the hypervisor, architecture and base OS whether you can do this. As I don't have access to an Arm Mac I cannot advise on what issues you may run into. (edited)
ThatGuy 2026-02-25 11:51 a.m.
Many thanks! I'll take a look!
PiecesAndPawns
I have an idea I'd like to get feedback on, before deciding if it should be raised as a "feature request" via github issues. Since a lot of people use a companion for multiple things (mqtt to letsmesh and other projects, meshcore-bot, home assistant, S.A.M.E weather alerts, and several other things.. it'd be awesome if we could have a single companion node (probably tied to HA), and let the other tools use it as a TCP connection, so that we don't need 11ty7 companions. Would it be possible AND, would it be of interest to create essentially a "tcp proxy" type of service in the HA integration, so the other tools could tie into it and use the same companion?
mrlm 2026-02-25 11:56 a.m.
I asked if this proxy idea would be possible at the start but there may be a problem with making it work how people may expect as the firmware is single connection only. Imagine MC-HA is connected and frequently requests the messages, at the same time another device connects in and modifies a contact. How would the responses be routed to the correct requestor? (edited)
PiecesAndPawns 2026-02-25 12:23 p.m.
@mrlm I was thinking more along the lines of HA would have the "direct connection" (BLE, serial, or TCP) to the companion, and expose a TCP port/proxy that other clients could connect to as sort of a "virtual companion", the service can handle sending data to/from the companion, and route the responses to the correct TCP connection
mrlm 2026-02-25 12:25 p.m.
Which may work for some tools, but the first thing people would try is the app or some existing tool which expects to make a direct unshared connection. I'm not saying its a bad idea or impossible just that it may be more complicated than it appears or lead to unexpected issues. I dont know. (edited)
Cybermans 2026-02-26 10:01 a.m.
I had like 3000 contacts in the discovered contacts file. It made the integration crash. Only thing I could was empty it. Which resulted in mentioning it being corrupted. What is the best way to prevent this
MAURO 2026-02-26 11:59 a.m.
Hello, good afternoon everyone. Is there a way to trigger a message on a channel using Home Assistant?
Cybermans
I had like 3000 contacts in the discovered contacts file. It made the integration crash. Only thing I could was empty it. Which resulted in mentioning it being corrupted. What is the best way to prevent this
mrlm 2026-02-26 12:34 p.m.
Do you mean this file /homeassistant/.storage/meshcore.01xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.discovered_contacts ?
mrlm
Do you mean this file /homeassistant/.storage/meshcore.01xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.discovered_contacts ?
Cybermans 2026-02-26 12:34 p.m.
Yes
mrlm 2026-02-26 12:37 p.m.
Delete it and a blank one will be created next time the integration starts, best to reboot HA though. I would assume it is tagged as corrupt because it is no longer a valid json file which would look something like this when empty. { "version": 1, "minor_version": 1, "key": "meshcore.01xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.discovered_contacts", "data": { ... } } } Mine is 29500 lines long and I have removed it recently while testing something, dont worry about a few entries. (edited)
MAURO
Hello, good afternoon everyone. Is there a way to trigger a message on a channel using Home Assistant?
mrlm 2026-02-26 12:48 p.m.
HA can do anything... Create an automation - Edit in YAML and paste this very basic one. Save/go back to see the GUI then choose an alternative trigger and channel idx alias: Meshcore - Message ahannel idx1 description: "" triggers: - trigger: time_pattern hours: /1 conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 1 message: Test message to Channel idx1 mode: single (edited)
mrlm
HA can do anything... Create an automation - Edit in YAML and paste this very basic one. Save/go back to see the GUI then choose an alternative trigger and channel idx alias: Meshcore - Message ahannel idx1 description: "" triggers: - trigger: time_pattern hours: /1 conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 1 message: Test message to Channel idx1 mode: single (edited)
MAURO 2026-02-26 1:30 p.m.
Perhaps I didn't explain my intention well. I have a group channel and I wanted that if someone sent a message there, for example, "!time", it would trigger an automation. I'm already using a method to send messages to the channel.
mrlm 2026-02-26 1:50 p.m.
I am glad you asked this as I have wanted to do it for ages on my private channel so I can see the route taken when I send a test message. Enable Developer Options in HA, go to Events, meshcore_message and start listening. Send a sample message of the type you want to detect, obviously MC-HA and the node need to be set up to receive this first. Go back to automations and create the new one using the information from the event. alias: Meshcore - reply to a specific message. description: "" triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: channel channel: "#test" channel_idx: 8 message: MRLM-HA? conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 8 message: Reply from MRLM-HA mode: single (edited)
mrlm
I am glad you asked this as I have wanted to do it for ages on my private channel so I can see the route taken when I send a test message. Enable Developer Options in HA, go to Events, meshcore_message and start listening. Send a sample message of the type you want to detect, obviously MC-HA and the node need to be set up to receive this first. Go back to automations and create the new one using the information from the event. alias: Meshcore - reply to a specific message. description: "" triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: channel channel: "#test" channel_idx: 8 message: MRLM-HA? conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 8 message: Reply from MRLM-HA mode: single (edited)
MAURO 2026-02-26 2:17 p.m.
Thank you so much, that's exactly what I needed.

mrlm
What are you wanting to do, HA can already monitor the repeater via the compnaion node, just not in real time. USB can be used for logging and control. The Observer firmware enable WiFi for MQTT to be sent out, it might not be much work to enable (edited)

mrlm 2026-03-03 10:15 a.m.
You can already monitor the repeater noise floor with HA, it can jump about as it is just not real time (5 mins) but may be good for what you are trying to do. I would look for a 15 minute period (three samples) where it stays higher than your threshold level. (edited)

mwolter 2026-04-01 10:29 p.m.
Hi Everyone, Working on a UI for meshcore-ha and would like your input before submitting it to the integration owner for consideration. Basically it’s a nice front end for the integration that makes it easy to manage messages, channels, contacts and your companion and router devices. All existing functionality, entities, logbooks, etc is maintained. It’s a bolt on front end that allows you to continue to use the automations and service for bots or other purposes. It's nice to be able to chat while out of the house and not having to be concerned bad coverage. Let me know if there are features you’re interested in and I’ll look into adding them! (edited)

PiecesAndPawns 2026-04-06 6:25 p.m.
I'm curious about the newer mqtt options. I've got a couple of mqtt servers enabled. I consistently see my node as "offline" in one of the more popular projects, but occasionally see it online. Is it expected that it drops the mqtt connection during periods of mesh inactivity, and reconnects when it hears an advert or something? Or, is this indicative of an issue?
PiecesAndPawns
I'm curious about the newer mqtt options. I've got a couple of mqtt servers enabled. I consistently see my node as "offline" in one of the more popular projects, but occasionally see it online. Is it expected that it drops the mqtt connection during periods of mesh inactivity, and reconnects when it hears an advert or something? Or, is this indicative of an issue?
mrlm 2026-04-07 3:48 a.m.
I have been sending MQTT from MC-HA to • Broker 1: meshrank.net (Enabled) • Broker 2: mqtt.ukmesh.com (Enabled) Both are working reliably as it was the top observer on both sites for ages. The MQTT data is such an insignificant amount compared to other things that it just gets on with it in the background. You clearly have an issue but it must not be how it is configured. Do you have another device you can set up as just an observer, to remove HA temporarilly? Is your ISP and WiFi / LAN stable? Is your HA overloaded? My RPI4-8GB was running HA fine untill MC-HA came along, then I noticed it was occasionally sluggish and the CPU was jumping up to 25% which was suspicious. I discovered that most of the HA / Python code only runs on one core, so the other three were no being used. Moved it to Intel / Proxmox VM and it was back to normal. (edited)
mrlm
I have been sending MQTT from MC-HA to • Broker 1: meshrank.net (Enabled) • Broker 2: mqtt.ukmesh.com (Enabled) Both are working reliably as it was the top observer on both sites for ages. The MQTT data is such an insignificant amount compared to other things that it just gets on with it in the background. You clearly have an issue but it must not be how it is configured. Do you have another device you can set up as just an observer, to remove HA temporarilly? Is your ISP and WiFi / LAN stable? Is your HA overloaded? My RPI4-8GB was running HA fine untill MC-HA came along, then I noticed it was occasionally sluggish and the CPU was jumping up to 25% which was suspicious. I discovered that most of the HA / Python code only runs on one core, so the other three were no being used. Moved it to Intel / Proxmox VM and it was back to normal. (edited)
PiecesAndPawns 2026-04-07 9:19 a.m.
Thanks for the ideas. I've confirmed from a hardware perspective that the HA host is not resource constrained. My network is stable (I have another observer on the same network that stays connected as expected, as I test the HA mqtt integration)

Very strange then, where are you seeing it as offline?
